fresh_facts.claim: Israeli Air Force conducted strikes on March 9-10 targeting IRGC Drone Command Headquarters, missile launchers, and multiple airbases including Isfahan's 8th Tactical Airbase.
Iran experienced its heaviest day of airstrikes on March 11, 2026 according to video reports.
IDF/IAF have cumulatively hit 10 of 17 Artesh Air Force bases, 3 of 6 aviation bases, and 6 airfields since February 28.
Approximately 1,900 Iranian regime members have been eliminated in US-Israeli strikes since February 28.
Iran's missile and drone capabilities were severely degraded, with IDF destroying 3 launchers used against Israel confirmed on March 9.
Iran launched 12 attack waves mostly targeting northern Israel on March 9 and 3 missile barrages from March 9 at 3:00 PM ET to March 10 at 8:00 AM ET.
IRGC Ground Forces dismantled an armed anti-regime group in Kurdistan Province on March 9, killing 1 and arresting 6.
Iran fired over 500 missiles and approximately 2,000 drones since February 28, reported on March 5.
US-Israeli strikes hit the 30th PMF Brigade in Ninewa, Iraq on March 9, with ongoing operations since March 2.
Iranian-backed militias launched drones and missiles at former US Victory Base and Baghdad Airport on March 8-9; these were intercepted by defenses.
US-Israeli strikes hit 11 or more Basij bases in Tehran, including the 15th Tehran Municipality Malik Ashtar base damaged per March 9 imagery.
A jailbreak occurred in Mariwan as a result of US-Israeli strikes on detention facilities, and the IRGC ordered Mariwan evacuation on March 3.
Mojtaba Khamenei was officially appointed Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts on March 8, 2026, following his father Ali Khamenei's assassination on February 28.
An elite counterterrorism unit (NOPO) has been deployed to protect Mojtaba Khamenei amid the ongoing war.
Hardline IRGC elements are reportedly dominating decision-making following the succession, with little evidence of internal fractures despite the leadership void.
Recent protest movements have been subject to harsh repression described as a 'dangerous suspension,' eroding regime legitimacy and creating political limbo.
ACLED assessed the US-Israeli campaign as degrading Iran's military and nuclear capabilities without achieving regime collapse as of March 2026.
fresh_facts.source: Armed Conflict Location & Event Data Project

fresh_facts.claim: Markets anticipated a quick end to the US-Israel war with Iran as of March 10, with investors betting on US President Trump's involvement despite mutual threats.
NCRI reports highlight Mojtaba Khamenei's alleged amassed fortune from oil wealth pillaging.
fresh_facts.source: National Council of Resistance of Iran

fresh_facts.claim: The US-Israel war with Iran is now in its 12th day as of March 11, 2026.
Israeli strikes targeted Quds Force headquarters, nuclear labs in Tehran, and multiple airbases including sites in Kerman, Khorramabad, Tabriz, and Karaj.
A second 'Shahid Soleimani' class IRGC vessel was struck at Bandar Abbas port during Israeli operations.

regime_fall.current_status: On day 12 of the US-Israeli campaign, Iran has sustained devastating military losses including destruction of 10 of 17 air force bases and approximately 1,900 regime members killed, while Mojtaba Khamenei was formally appointed Supreme Leader on March 8 following his father's assassination, with hardline IRGC elements consolidating control despite severe internal instability and protest suppression.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Mojtaba Khamenei was officially appointed Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts on March 8, 2026, following Ali Khamenei's assassination on February 28, establishing a new leadership during the ongoing US-Israeli military campaign.
